overview: This step creates translations for a raw document by delegating the actual translation to an external program (e.g., Apertium, ProMT). It extracts text units into a temporary HTML document, invokes the external tool to translate it, then aligns the source and translated segments into TM entries. The results can be stored in a Pensieve TM, exported as TMX, or both. The original raw document is passed through unmodified to the next step (unless TMX forwarding is enabled).

command.help:
The command line that invokes the external translation tool. The step creates a temporary HTML document where each paragraph is a source segment (or unsegmented text unit). Your command must read from `${inputPath}` and write the translated HTML to `${outputPath}`.

Available variables:

| Variable | Description |
|---|---|
| `${inputPath}` | Full path of the temporary input HTML |
| `${inputURI}` | URI of the temporary input HTML |
| `${outputPath}` | Full path where translated HTML must be written |
| `${outputURI}` | URI of the expected output HTML |
| `${srcLangName}` | English name of source language (e.g., `German` for `de-ch`) |
| `${trgLangName}` | English name of target language (e.g., `Japanese` for `ja-jp`) |
| `${rootDir}` | Project/batch root directory |
| `${inputRootDir}` | Root directory of the input files |

All standard locale variables (`${srcLang}`, `${trgLang}`, `${srcLoc}`, `${trgLoc}`, etc.) are also available.
command.values: A shell command string with `${variable}` placeholders. The external tool **must** produce output at `${outputPath}` in the same HTML format as `${inputPath}`.

origin.help: An optional identifier string attached to each translated entry as an `Origin` property. In TMX output this appears as `<prop type="Txt::Origin">yourValue</prop>`.
examples: `Apertium-MT` — identifies translations as coming from Apertium
`ProMT-v9` — identifies translations from a specific ProMT version

markAsMT.help: Flags all generated TM entries as machine translation results. When enabled, the `creationId` attribute of each target entry is set to `"MT!"`.

makeTM.help: Imports translated entries into a Pensieve TM. Entries are indexed at the end of each input document, so downstream steps can only access entries generated from **previous** documents in the batch — not the current one.

tmDirectory.help:
Directory of the Pensieve TM to populate. If the TM does not exist, it will be created. If it already exists, new entries are appended.

Supports `${rootDir}`, `${inputRootDir}`, and all locale variables in the path.

makeTMX.help: Creates a single TMX document containing translated entries from **all** input documents in the batch. The file is not written until the very last document is processed, so it **cannot** be consumed by downstream steps during the pipeline run.

tmxPath.help:
Full path of the TMX file to generate. If the file already exists, it will be **overwritten**.

Supports `${rootDir}`, `${inputRootDir}`, and all locale variables in the path.

sendTMX.help: Replaces the normal pipeline output. Instead of passing the original raw document to the next step, the generated TMX document is sent as a raw document.

dependsOn.condition: must be true for TMX to exist

notes: When enabled, the original input document is **not** forwarded to downstream steps.

blockSize.help: Controls how many text units are batched into each temporary HTML file sent to the external tool. This enables processing of very large documents even with tools that have size limitations — each block is translated independently and results are reassembled.
blockSize.values: Positive integer. Default `1000` text units per block.

checkExistingTm.help: Before sending a text unit for translation, checks whether a translation already exists in a Pensieve TM. Matching entries are **skipped** — they are neither re-translated nor included in the optional TMX output. This avoids redundant translation work and cost.

existingTm.help:
Directory of the Pensieve TM to use for lookups. This is a **read-only** lookup — entries are not modified or added here.

Supports `${rootDir}`, `${inputRootDir}`, and all locale variables in the path.

segment.help:
Segments text units into sentences using SRX rules before creating the temporary HTML. When enabled, each HTML paragraph contains one sentence. When disabled, each paragraph is an unsegmented text unit (typically a full paragraph).

Segmentation affects what the external tool sees and translates — sentence-level segments often produce better MT output.

srxPath.help:
Full path to the SRX segmentation rules file.

Supports `${rootDir}`, `${inputRootDir}`, and all locale variables in the path.

processingNotes: The step extracts content from the raw document into a temporary HTML file, invokes the external tool, then aligns source/translated segments — the original document passes through **unmodified**.
Large documents are split into blocks (controlled by **blockSize**), each translated independently, allowing tools with size limits to handle arbitrarily large inputs.
Pensieve TM entries are indexed at the end of each document — downstream steps can only query entries from previously processed documents, not the current one.
TMX output is written only after the **last** input document is processed, so it is unavailable to downstream steps during the run.
Entries that already exist in a checked TM are skipped entirely and are **not** copied into the TMX output.

examples.title: Apertium MT on Linux
examples.description: Translates documents using the open-source Apertium machine translation system. The `${srcLang}-${trgLang}` variable pair selects the language direction (e.g., `es-en`).
examples.config:
```yaml
command: "apertium -f html ${srcLang}-${trgLang} ${inputPath} ${outputPath}"
blockSize: 1000
markAsMT: true
makeTMX: true
tmxPath: "${rootDir}/output/${srcLang}-${trgLang}.tmx"
```

examples.title: ProMT on Windows
examples.description: Uses the ProMT commercial translation engine via its FileTranslator CLI. Language direction uses full English language names (e.g., `English-German`).
examples.config:
```yaml
command: '"C:\Program Files\PRMT9\FILETRANS\FileTranslator.exe" ${inputPath} /as /ac /d:${srcLangName}-${trgLangName} /o:${outputPath}'
blockSize: 500
markAsMT: true
origin: "ProMT-v9"
makeTM: true
tmDirectory: "${rootDir}/tm"
```
